task_completion.description: Open Epi Info 7 Visual Dashboard and load the diabetes_surveillance.csv dataset
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Epi Info 7 Visual Dashboard application is open and the data source 'diabetes_surveillance.csv' is shown as loaded in the interface.

task_completion.description: Add a scatter plot gadget to the dashboard canvas
task_completion.visual_evidence: A scatter plot gadget or window is visible within the Epi Info 7 Visual Dashboard workspace.

task_completion.description: Configure the scatter plot with BMI on the Y-axis and Glucose on the X-axis
task_completion.visual_evidence: The scatter plot gadget properties or the plot itself clearly shows 'BMI' assigned to the Y-axis (vertical) and 'Glucose' assigned to the X-axis (horizontal).

task_completion.description: Identify the extreme outlier's PatientID and BMI value
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the extreme outlier point on the scatter plot (e.g., hovering over it to see a tooltip) or views the underlying data table to reveal PatientID 450 and BMI 175.6.

task_completion.description: Save the dashboard to the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or file explorer showing the dashboard being saved as 'qa_dashboard.canvas7' (or 'qa_dashboard.cvs7') in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\DiabetesData' directory.

task_completion.description: Create the outlier_report.txt containing the PatientID and erroneous BMI
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like Notepad) is open showing '450' and '175.6' being saved to 'outlier_report.txt' in the DiabetesData folder, or a file explorer showing the created text file.

integrity.description: The agent used the Epi Info 7 GUI to perform the analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Epi Info 7 Visual Dashboard interface being actively used to load data and generate the plot, rather than using a Python script or terminal commands to find the outlier.

integrity.description: The agent actually generated the scatter plot to find the outlier
integrity.visual_evidence: The scatter plot with BMI and Glucose must be visible in the screenshots before the final report is created, proving the agent didn't just guess or hardcode the known outlier values.

integrity.description: The agent created the report file using standard desktop tools
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., Notepad) or standard terminal echo commands should be visible creating the report, rather than a script bypassing the GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains a deliberately injected extreme outlier: PatientID 450 has an impossible BMI value of 175.6 and a Glucose value of 125. The scatter plot must be configured with BMI on the Y-axis and Glucose on the X-axis. Note: The task description asks to save the dashboard as 'qa_dashboard.canvas7', but Epi Info 7 natively uses the '.cvs7' extension. Accept either extension (.canvas7 or .cvs7) as long as the dashboard is saved in the correct 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\DiabetesData' directory. The final report 'outlier_report.txt' must contain the values '450' and '175.6'.
